---
jupyter: python3
---

# sxAct — Getting Started (Python)

> **Note:** Auto-generated from `notebooks/python/basics.qmd` via Quarto.
> Edit the source `.qmd` file, not the `.ipynb`.

## Overview

This notebook walks through the same workflow as the Julia version,
using the sxAct Python adapter. Under the hood, all computation
is performed by the Julia engine via `juliacall`.

**Requirements:** Python 3.10+, Julia 1.12+.

## 1. Setup

```{python}
# Uncomment if running on Google Colab:
# !pip install sxact

from sxact.adapter import julia_stub as jl
```

## 2. Define a Manifold

A 4-dimensional spacetime manifold with abstract indices.

```{python}
jl.execute("DefManifold", name="M", dim=4, indices=["a", "b", "c", "d", "e", "f"])
```

## 3. Define a Metric

Lorentzian signature, covariant derivative CD.
Automatically creates Riemann, Ricci, Weyl, Einstein, Christoffel.

```{python}
jl.execute("DefMetric", signature=-1, metric_expr="g[-a,-b]", covd_name="CD")
```

## 4. Canonicalization

Symmetric metric: $g_{ba} - g_{ab} = 0$.

```{python}
result = jl.execute("ToCanonical", expr="g[-b,-a] - g[-a,-b]")
print(result)
```

Define a symmetric tensor and canonicalize:

```{python}
jl.execute(
    "DefTensor",
    name="T",
    indices=["-a", "-b"],
    manifold="M",
    symmetry_str="Symmetric[{-a,-b}]",
)
result = jl.execute("ToCanonical", expr="T[-b,-a] - T[-a,-b]")
print(result)
```

## 5. Contraction

Lower an index with the metric:

```{python}
jl.execute("DefTensor", name="V", indices=["a"], manifold="M")
result = jl.execute("Contract", expr="V[a] * g[-a,-b]")
print(result)
```

## 6. Riemann Identities

First Bianchi identity:

```{python}
result = jl.execute(
    "ToCanonical",
    expr="RiemannCD[-a,-b,-c,-d] + RiemannCD[-a,-c,-d,-b] + RiemannCD[-a,-d,-b,-c]",
)
print(result)
```

Pair symmetry — $R_{abcd} = R_{cdab}$:

```{python}
result = jl.execute(
    "ToCanonical",
    expr="RiemannCD[-a,-b,-c,-d] - RiemannCD[-c,-d,-a,-b]",
)
print(result)
```

## 7. Perturbation Theory

```{python}
jl.execute(
    "DefTensor",
    name="h",
    indices=["-a", "-b"],
    manifold="M",
    symmetry_str="Symmetric[{-a,-b}]",
)
jl.execute("DefPerturbation", tensor="h", background="g", order=1)
result = jl.execute("Perturb", expr="g[-a,-b]", order=1)
print(result)
```

## Next Steps

- Full Julia API for more advanced workflows
- Coordinate components, covariant derivative commutation
- Documentation: [sashakile.github.io/sxAct](https://sashakile.github.io/sxAct/)
